data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" → 33d0 AC1 (exactly one outbound applier path remains in production; retired path deleted, no dead separately-tested applier code). FULLY.
- Parent AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" → 33d0 AC1 (deletion) + 33d0 AC2 (behavioral-parity gate verified BEFORE deletion — surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did). FULLY.
- Parent SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 AC1 + AC3 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — driven through the production path, each sub-op asserted). FULLY.
- CONTRIBUTED: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via 33d0 AC4; parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 33d0 AC5 (asserts effects/sub-op outcomes, never interaction/mock-call-counts).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "fetcher snapshot validated vs schema" + AC "each differ's reads validated vs schema" + AC "one round-trip contract through the production path w/ real fixtures" + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on shape" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3; confirmed owns _snapshot_schema.py + test_snapshot_contract.py) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake fidelity).
- AC "sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly (after warn-first rollout)" + SC "silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary" → 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3; depends_on 33d0 declared).
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a.

ANTI-FP — parent AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" is FULLY covered, NOT an absence. The AC verbatim names its owner (story B = sibling 8937), and 8937 owns it (description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact …" + 8937 AC5 "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note …) is committed and referenced by the new tests"; file_impact docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md). Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered. The earlier recurring "AC6 governs-new-tests PARTIALLY covered" finding was raised against paraphrased wording that does not match the plan text and is withdrawn across the roster; not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY (owned by THIS child) — parent "collapse to single production path", parent "retired path deleted (with parity gate)", parent SC "production path exercised, not dead path"; CONTRIBUTED — parent "suite green" + parent SC "zero change-detector"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — schema/contract/round-trip (8937/fe57/59ef), sub-op telemetry/canary (2359), bidirectional round-trip/probe de-encoding (822a), documented guardrail (8937 = story B, named owner); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent "all six closed; full reconciler suite green". UNCOVERED — none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and THIS child fully delivers its owned share (applier collapse, retired-path deletion with the parity-before-delete gate, production-path exercise).
